@graph.definition: Examples include vibrating truck and explosives.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Clearly defined administrative areas of a political, natural, or regulatory boundary nature, excluding scientific speculative boundaries such as geological boundaries, ecological / ecosystem boundaries etc.  Examples include coastlines (because coastlines define certain administrative boundaries), state boundaries, electoral boundaries, mineral tenement boundaries, petroleum title boundaries and marine protected areas.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Airborne Electromagnetics surveys
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Photographs of a part of the earth's surface taken by a camera mounted in an aircraft for mapping purposes. This usually consists of a series of overlapping vertical photos taken in strips which can form the basis for mapping.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Pertains to both marine and freshwater ecosystems. For example, reefs
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Data describing aquifer properties. Porous, water-bearing layers of sand, gravel, and rock below the earth's surface; reservoirs for groundwater.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Radioactive isotope dating technique
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Includes sub-surface distribution of metamorphic, sedimentary and igneous rocks and interpreted linear structural features such as folds, faults, lineaments and shearzones
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Measurements of ocean depth and the charting of the topography of the ocean floor.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The benthic zone is the ecological region at the lowest level of a body of water such as an ocean or a lake, including the sediment surface and some sub-surface layers.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Stratigraphy based on the paleontologic aspects of rocks.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: A borehole is any narrow shaft drilled in the ground, either vertically or horizontally, it includes Petroleum Wells, Stratigraphic Holes, Mineral Holes, Geothermal Holes, Water Bores
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: A public record of the extent, value, and ownership of land within a district for purposes of taxation.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The use of non-radiogenic dating studies such as U-Pb ratios
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: A Coal Seam Methane hole is drilled specifically to discover, asses or produce from a coal seam methane reservoir. EXAMPLE UPDATE.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: AEM, ground EM, Point located data, grids, derivative grids, algorithms, images, sections, volumes
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The density of a material is defined as its mass per unit volume.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Dissolved solids" refer to any minerals, salts, metals, cations or anions dissolved in water.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Satellite and air platform imagery. This includes imagery designed to represent surficial condition of the earth such as Landsat, SPOT, MODIS, ASTER, HYMAP and Aerial Photography. Remotely sensed data acquired from earth observation satellites and Sensing Instruments for both scientific and operational applications, such as LANDSAT 5,LANDSAT 7,RADARSAT 1 (Synthetic Aperture Radar - SAR), ERS 2 (Synthetic Aperture Radar - SAR) TERRA Modis,AQUA Modis, NOAA 17, SPOT, ASTER, HYMAP and Aerial Photography
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: An earthquake is the shaking and vibration at the surface of the earth caused by underground movement along a fault plane or by volcanic activity.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Elevation data such as Digital Elevation Models (DEMs).
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Electron Microprobe
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Defined areas for the purposes of oil and mineral exploitation
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: External datasets sourced from other Agencies
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: This includes for example navigational data for seismic surveys, landsat path row diagrams and field mapping survey and site information including field geology observations and measurements.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Measurements taken at a field site such as structural, spectral (PIMA) or geophysical (magnetic susceptibility)
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Visual observations and photos of the vegetation, landscape and geomorphology at a field site and descriptions of the mineralogy, lithology, alteration of rocks, samples and outcrops at a field site.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Hydraulic conductivity, porosity, permeability, specific discharge
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Data related to the chemical analyses and composition of earth materials. This includes information related to the chemical analysis of organic material, surficial, groundwater and pore water samples, benthic chemical analysis, and stable isotope studies
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Age approximations of earth materials such as rocks, sediments and minerals. Age data resulting from scientific techniques such as radio isotopes and chemical dating.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Measurements and representation of the earth, its gravitational field and geodynamic phenomena (polar motion, earth tides, and crustal motion) in three-dimensional time varying space.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The study of the origin of landscapes based on a cause and effect relationship. This includes the physical and chemical interactions between the earth's surface and the natural forces acting on it, such as geology, climate, vegetation and relative base level.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Includes seismic, gravity and airborne survey locations. For example, seismic navigation data.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Interpretation using geophysical techniques and data
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Data of the physical characteristics of the earth (crust, mantle, core)  such as gravity, magnetics, radiometrics, and seismic. This also includes information relating to basement and crustal studies.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: A Geothermal hole is drilled specifically for the exploration, development, appraisal or monitoring of geothermal plays or resources
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Including ground, airborne, point located data, grids, derivative grids (reduced to pole, upward continued, etc), algorithms, images
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Ground based Electromagnetics surveys
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Natural  and human environmental phenomena, whether slow or fast acting.  Information on the natural environment and its interaction with geology, eg waste disposal and pollution such as geological disposal of CO2.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Includes groundwater, porewater and surface water
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Hydroacoustic sensing involves "passive acoustics" (listening for sounds) or active acoustics making a sound and listening for the echo, hence the common name for the device, echo sounder or echosounder.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Oil and gas production
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The distribution and movement of groundwater and dissolved materials in the soil and rocks of the Earth's crust (commonly in aquifers).
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Information related to the distribution,  mapping and physical characteristics of surficial waters. Examples include the locations of bores, lakes, dams, canals, sea levels etc,
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Infrasound is sound that is lower in frequency than 20 cycles per second, the normal limit of human hearing. The study of such sound waves is sometimes referred to as infrasonics, covering sounds beneath 20 Hz down to 0.001 Hz. This frequency range is utilized for monitoring earthquakes, charting rock and petroleum formations below the earth
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Any structure fixed to a terrestrial or marine location to facilitate commercial or daily activities, or the supply of essential services. Examples include processing plants / factories, ports, oil rigs etc. This includes information regarding the facilitation of transporting  people or commodities.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Describing a substance not derived from a living organism and/or not composed of carbon and hydrogen (a hydrocarbon).
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Information related to the interpretation of  baseline geoscientific information integrated with field and petrographic observations and measurements. Examples include the interpretation of geological provinces, outcrop and basement geology, 3D geology models, geophysical imagery stratigraphic cross-sections.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Radioactive isotope dating technique
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: LIDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) is an optical remote sensing technology that measures properties of scattered light to find range and/or other information of a distant target. The prevalent method to determine distance to an object or surface is to use laser pulses. Like the similar radar technology, which uses radio waves instead of light, the range to an object is determined by measuring the time delay between transmission of a pulse and detection of the reflected signal.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Magnetic Susceptibility is the degree of magnetization of a material in response to an applied magnetic field.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Point located data, grids, derivative grids (reduced to pole, upward continued, etc), algorithms, images
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Manufacturing is the transformation, by means of a tool and/or processing medium, of raw materials into finished goods for sale, or intermediate processes involving the production or finishing of semi-manufactures. It is a large branch of industry and of secondary production.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Zones of similar mineralogy caused by heat and pressure
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Structural geology using microscopes
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Includes mineral occurrences, resources, deposits, mineral potential, mineral deposit models, prospectivity maps
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: A Mineral hole is drilled for the exploration, development or resource definition of mineral commodities
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Geographical boundaries such as coastlines
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The distribution of plants, animals; natural biotic distribution patterns and habitats for aerial, terrestrial, marine, and other life.BIOME: are distinct ecological communities of plants and animals living together in a particular climate. Scientists classify biomes in various ways, but the major biomes include aquatic, deserts, forests, grasslands, and tundra.?
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Optical remote sensing makes use of visible, near infrared and short-waveinfrared sensors to form images of the earth's surface by detecting thesolar radiation reflected from targets on the ground. Different materials reflect and absorb differently at different wavelengths. Thus, the targets can be differentiated by their spectral reflectance signatures in the remotely sensed images. Optical remote sensing systems are classified into the following types, depending on the number of spectral bands used in the imaging process.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Material  derived from living organisms and is made up of carbon-based compounds.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Interpretation of the nature and distribution of outcropping (or near-outcropping) rock units and structures, typically in the form of maps.  These maps may include integrated regolith unit information in areas of non-outcropping bedrock.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The climate of a given interval of time in the the geologic past.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: An environment in the geologic past
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Palinspastic reconstructions or geological time-slice interpretations
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The study of life in past geologic time, based on fossil plants and animals. Chronology of the Earth's history.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Passive geophysical surveys are ones that incorporate measurements of naturally occurring fields or properties of the earth.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Petrographic observations and measurements such as mineralogy, lithology, alteration, mineralisation, metamorphic grade, weathering and grainsize in specimens and thin sections (micro scale).
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: A Petroleum Well is drilled for the exploration, development or appraisal of hydrocarbons (oil or gas)
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Includes thin section, fluid inclusion observations and measurements of hand specimens from the surface or down boreholes
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Data related to the physical expression of the earths surface either terrestrial or marine. Examples include marine bathymetric information, seafloor expressions (mounts, slopes, shelf, rise) freshwater/estuarine bed profiles and  Digital Elevation Models
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: State , electoral and native boundaries
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Porosity and Permeability are two of the primary factors that control the movement and storage of fluids in rocks and sediments. They are intrinsic characteristics of these geologic materials. The exploitation of natural resources, such as groundwater and petroleum, is partly dependent on the properties of porosity and permeability.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Descriptions and polygon outlines of geological provinces of the Australian continent and surrounding offshore regions.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: RADAR stands for "RAdio Detection And Ranging". By virtue of sending out pulses of microwave electromagnetic radiation this type of instrument can be classified as an "active sensor" - it measures the time between pulses and their reflected components to determine distance. Different pulse intervals, different wavelengths, different geometry and polarizations can be combined to roughness characteristics of the earth surface.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Radioactive isotopes
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Point located data, grids, derivative grids (filtered, etc), algorithms, images
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Radioactive isotope dating technique
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Everything above fresh rock
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Legal boundaries like mining tenements and cadastre
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Data on resource production and/or commodity potential or information. Examples include minerals and petroleum potential, oil and gas fields information, resource and production tonnage and grade information, deposit types and mineralisation styles
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Physical measurements of rock properties such as density and susceptibility for use in predictive modelling studies such as seismic predictions, geophysical inversions, and groundwater distribution, or engineering work.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Contains the sampling locations of features which were the target for a set of observations and/or measurements.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: SEGY, seismic profiles, images, processed data, weak and strong motion from seismographs and accelerators
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Radioactive isotope dating technique
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Environmental events associated with earth processes that impact Australian communities such as salinity, acid-sulphate soils, coastal erosion, desertification, climate change
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Radioactive isotope dating technique
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: An Anthropogenic feature to facilitate cultural, societal or religious activities eg cemeteries, recreational areas, land uses related to primary industries,  building infrastructure etc. Excludes information relating to transportation.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Similar data to other geophysical types, but in the "spectral geology" space, with many purposes. For example, PIMA and Hylogger
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: For example, delta 18O/16O
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: A Stratigraphic hole is drilled to obtain a record of the Stratigraphic character, composition or property of the rock/soil/regolith units penetrated
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Sudden events associated with earth processes that impact Australian communities such as earthquake, landslide, flood, tsunami, wildfire, tropical cyclone, storm surge, severe windstorm
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Examples include potentiometric and phreatic surfaces
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Information regarding the location, purpose and methodology related to particular surveys of any kind. This includes, for example navigational data for seismic surveys, landsat path row diagrams. This category can be summarised as metadata regarding any type of survey undertaken or managed within the Organisation.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The theory and practice of classifying plants and animals.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: For example, land vegetation - pest and native flora and fauna
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Sudden emergency events associated with acts of terrorism causing damage to human life or the economy
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Geological datasets developed for a specific purpose, such as "Volcanoes of the World"
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Remote sensing of direct temperature effects is carried out by sensing radiation emitted from matter in the thermal infrared region of the spectrum.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Thermal properties of fluids, gases and solids
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: The shapes, patterns and physical configuration of the surface of the land, including its relief (local differences in elevation) and the positions of natural and man-made features.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Data relating to any infrastructure facilitating the movement of people or goods by motorised means. This includes roads, railways and flight paths, aerodromes etc.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Radioactive isotope dating technique
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Information relating to the supply or provision of utility services such as electricity, gas, steam, water or sewage. Examples include oil , gas , electricity and water pipelines.
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: A Water Bore is drilled specifically for the exploration, extraction or monitoring of groundwater resources
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme

@graph.definition: Reflected waves can be generated from a planar boundary in seismic wave speed that can be recorded at the Earth's surface
@graph.schemeLabel: Legacy GA Corporate Data Classification Scheme
